title: The decades-long subversive capture of the Constitutional State by agents of the Deep State in Sri Lanka
body_lines: Two extraordinary developments this week may be of interest to Sri Lanka watchers familiar with the decades-long subversive capture of the Constitutional State by agents of the Deep State.

First, the Bar Association of Sri Lanka’s expression of strongly worded concern to the Chief Justice and the Attorney General this Friday, regarding ‘a concerted effort by certain parties’ to discredit the judiciary and the Bar by ‘falsely accusing judges and members of the legal profession of promoting violence,’ is significant.

Extraordinarily it was warned that ‘these elements,’ (unnamed as the Bar preferred to be the case apparently), had gone so far as to cast ‘aspersions on orders made by judicial officers in pending cases relating to protests.’The Bar has asked for these ‘unnamed elements’ to be dealt with for contempt of court in terms of Article 105 of the Constitution.

The Attorney General was also asked to prosecute any responsible for unlawful interference with the judiciary under Article 111C of the Constitution. There is, of course, an immediate and patent irony here.

As we may recall, an Opposition Member of Parliament was sent to jail for four years for committing contempt as a result of ad hoc comments loosely categorising ‘a majority’ of judges, lawyers in Sri Lanka as corrupt. He is still serving his term.

In contrast, how much more gravity attaches to aspersions cast by ‘unnamed elements’ on judicial officers weighing the right to protest and the right to speech and expression of citizens in the scales of public order? Judges, mainly of the subordinate courts, have been performing these functions in an impeccably proper manner during the past tumultuous weeks. Every ounce of judicial discretion and restraint has been called for, as the nation tipped over into chaos following unprovoked assaults of peaceful protestors by political goons.

Judicial interventions have prevented violence in some instances while full scale civil conflict in this country has been avoided in others. Most importantly, this has strengthened public confidence in the court system and the ability of both judges and lawyers to stand up to violations of the Constitution. That has been crucial as the political command (the Office of the President and the Office of the Prime Minister) not only abdicated its role in preventing the violence in the first instance but also became acutely complicit in the same.

So from what springs this need to ‘cast aspersions’ on judicial officers performing their role? Quite apart from contempt powers under Article 105, the historic import of Article 111C in making interference with the judiciary, an offence, speaks to precisely such a situation. That Article, in sub article (1) is to the effect that every judge, presiding officer, public officer or interalia, other persons entrusted by law with judicial powers or functions shall not be subjected to direction or other interference proceeding from ‘any other person’ other than interalia, a superior court.

Article 111C (2) says that any person who, without ‘legal authority’ interferes in the manner prohibited previously, may be punishable by the High Court on a jail term of one year, a fine as well as the imposition of civic disabilities. Its consequences are therefore as severe or even more so than an offence of contempt. Citizens of Sri Lanka would like to know exactly why the full and awful authority of the law has not been brought against these ‘elements’ attempting to coerce judicial officers? The Attorney General of Sri Lanka must answer to that question, no doubt.
What sanctity attaches to ‘unnamed elements’ casting aspersions on judges?

For if a Member of Parliament is punished by the law, along with critics and others accused of contempt at various times, what sanctity attaches to ‘unnamed elements’ violating the Constitution far more egregiously? Who is the protector of such ‘elements’? Put bluntly,  warnings issued to judicial officers in regard to performing their judicial role underpins the culture of impunity, bolstered by military officers who President Gotabaya Rajapaksa has seen fit to appoint to offices across the civil administration spectrum.

We welcomed that military ‘capture’ of the State, eyes wide shut, as models of competency and discipline, soon to learn bitterly to the contrary. Even so, overt threats to judges handling ‘protest cases’ is another inglorious first in Sri Lanka’s post-independence judicial history. This follows close upon the disgraceful impeachment of a sitting Chief Justice akin to a common criminal during the second term of the Mahinda Rajapaksa Presidency (2013).
For those less acquainted with the travails of this nation’s judicial system, it must also be reminded that the Rajapaksas are not wholesale responsible for this state.

The deterioration of the judicial institution increased pace under the Chandrika Kumaratunga Presidency with the Office of the Chief Justice attracting deeply negative scrutiny by global judicial monitors including the International Bar Association (IBA). More recently, the Sirisena-Wickremesinghe regime abandoned a golden opportunity to restore the Constitutional State, preferring to play political power games with the 19th Amendment to the Constitution.

So when the current Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe pronounces with a flourish to the international media that the present Government is responsible for Sri Lanka’s financial crisis, he is only partly right.

The catalogue of misdeeds traceable to his leadership during 2015-2019 are long and horrific. Like all Sri Lankan politicians, this Prime Minister too is a veritable ostrich, burying his head in the sand. Granting that, Rajapaksa excesses have nevertheless, the air of nasty brutish power behind them, reflecting the Leviathan State in its most violent and negative form.

Anarchy in this case, is propelled by the State. That is again exemplified by the fact that days before the BASL missives, a letter by the Inspector General of Police (IGP) to the Secretary of the Public Security Ministry was strategically leaked to the media.

Outlining the disastrous breakdown of Sri Lanka’s policing command structures, the IGP informed that 120 police officers have been appointed as Officers-in-Charge of Police Stations without appearing for interviews. There is more to this. 3 officers who did appear at the interviews but were not recommended for promotion, had been appointed regardless and those who had been appointed to key stations (unsurprisingly) lacked experience.

These disclosures link up to questions being asked regarding the extent to which hidden subversive hands were behind the burning of Minsters’ houses, the attacks on politicians and property two weeks ago.

These questions will go unanswered as much as the failure to identify the ‘maha molakaru’ (mastermind) of the 2019 Easter Sunday blowing up of churches and hotels, carried out by ‘homegrown’ jihadists. All of these puzzles are tied up intricately with true skill, requiring the peeling away of many layers of a rotten onion as it were. We can only predict with certainty that assurances of justice for civilian victims by the chiefs of law enforcement, the state prosecutor’s department and ubiquitous Commissions of Inquiry will be in vain. Ministers and politicians will however be compensated.

Even now as the State retreats from its basic functions, these lessons of the Rule of Law have not been learnt. Blustering about, like ‘bulls in a china shop’ where the nation’s judiciary is concerned does not reflect well on a Government pleading with international lenders to obviate the consequences of a desperate bankruptcy.
This fate was brought upon the citizenry by a crooked, greedy and communally manipulative political command coupled with our profound inability to see the devil for what it is.
Rein in your club-wielding and letter-writing goons, this Government must be told in no uncertain terms.

si.title: ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ගැඹුරු රාජ්‍යයේ නියෝජිතයන් විසින් ව්‍යවස්ථාමය රාජ්‍යය දශක ගණනාවක් තිස්සේ කඩාකප්පල්කාරී ලෙස අල්ලා ගැනීම
body_lines: ගැඹුරු රාජ්‍යයේ නියෝජිතයන් විසින් දශක ගණනාවක් තිස්සේ ව්‍යවස්ථාමය රාජ්‍යය කඩාකප්පල්කාරී ලෙස අල්ලා ගැනීම පිළිබඳව හුරුපුරුදු ශ්‍රී ලංකා නිරීක්ෂකයින්ට මෙම සතියේ අසාමාන්‍ය වර්ධනයන් දෙකක් උනන්දු විය හැකිය.

පළමුව, ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමය විසින් මෙම සිකුරාදා අගවිනිසුරුවරයාට සහ නීතිපතිවරයාට දැඩි ලෙස කනස්සල්ල පළ කරමින්, 'විනිසුරුවරුන්ට සහ නීති සාමාජිකයින්ට බොරු චෝදනා කරමින් අධිකරණය සහ නීතිඥ මණ්ඩලය අපකීර්තියට පත් කිරීමට ඇතැම් පාර්ශ්වවල ඒකාබද්ධ උත්සාහයක්' සම්බන්ධයෙන් ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය ප්‍රවර්ධනය කිරීමේ වෘත්තිය,' සැලකිය යුතු ය.

අසාමාන්‍ය ලෙස අනතුරු අඟවා ඇත්තේ මෙම මූලද්‍රව්‍ය, (පෙනෙන ලෙසට එසේ වීමට කැමති ලෙස නම් නොකළ) 'විරෝධතා සම්බන්ධයෙන් විභාග වෙමින් පවතින නඩු වලදී අධිකරණ නිලධාරීන් විසින් කරන ලද නියෝගවලට අවනත නොවන ලෙස' දක්වා දුරදිග ගොස් ඇති බවයි. ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 105 වැනි වගන්තිය ප්‍රකාරව අධිකරණයට අපහාස කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් ක්‍රියා කළ යුතු මෙම 'නම් නොකළ අංග' සඳහා.

ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 111 සී ව්‍යවස්ථාව යටතේ අධිකරණයට නීතිවිරෝධී මැදිහත්වීම් සම්බන්ධයෙන් වගකිව යුතු ඕනෑම අයෙකුට නඩු පවරන ලෙසද නීතිපතිවරයාගෙන් ඉල්ලා සිටියේය. ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම, මෙහි ක්ෂණික සහ පේටන්ට් උත්ප්‍රාසයක් ඇත.

අපට මතක ඇති පරිදි, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ 'බහුතරයක්' විනිසුරුවරුන්, නීතීඥයින් දූෂිතයන් ලෙස ලිහිල් ලෙස වර්ගීකරණය කරමින් තත්‍යානුකූල අදහස් දැක්වීමේ ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස විපක්ෂ මන්ත්‍රීවරයෙකු අපහාස කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් වසර හතරකට සිරගත කරන ලදී. ඔහු තවමත් ඔහුගේ ධුර කාලය ගෙවමින් සිටී.

ඊට ප්‍රතිවිරුද්ධව, මහජන සාමයේ තරාදියේ පුරවැසියන්ගේ විරෝධතා දැක්වීමේ අයිතිය සහ කතා කිරීමේ සහ ප්‍රකාශ කිරීමේ අයිතිය කිරා මැන බලන අධිකරණ නිලධාරීන්ට ‘නම් නොකළ අංග’ විසින් එල්ල කරන ලද අභිලාෂයන් සඳහා කොපමණ ගුරුත්වාකර්ෂණයක් බැඳී තිබේද? ප්‍රධාන වශයෙන් යටත් උසාවිවල විනිසුරුවරුන් පසුගිය කලබලකාරී සති කිහිපය තුළ මෙම කාර්යයන් පරිපූර්ණ ලෙස නිසි ලෙස ඉටු කළහ. දේශපාලන මැරයන් විසින් සාමකාමී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට ප්‍රකෝපකාරී ලෙස ප්‍රහාර එල්ල කිරීමෙන් පසු ජාතිය අවුල් සහගත තත්ත්වයකට පත්ව ඇති හෙයින්, සෑම අවුන්සයක්ම අධිකරණ අභිමතය සහ සංයමය ඉල්ලා ඇත.

අධිකරණ මැදිහත්වීම් මගින් ඇතැම් අවස්ථාවල ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය වැළැක්වී ඇති අතර තවත් අවස්ථා වලදී මේ රටේ පූර්ණ පරිමාණ සිවිල් ගැටුම් වළක්වා ඇත. වැදගත්ම දෙය නම්, මෙමගින් අධිකරණ පද්ධතිය කෙරෙහි මහජන විශ්වාසය ශක්තිමත් වී ඇති අතර ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාව උල්ලංඝණය කිරීම්වලට එරෙහිව නැගී සිටීමට විනිසුරුවරුන්ට සහ නීතීඥයින්ට ඇති හැකියාවයි. දේශපාලන විධානය (ජනාධිපති කාර්යාලය සහ අගමැති කාර්යාලය) ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය වැලැක්වීමේ සිය කාර්යභාරය මුල් අවස්ථාවේදීම අත්හැර දැමුවා පමණක් නොව, ඊට උග්‍ර ලෙස හවුල් වීම නිසා එය තීරණාත්මක විය.

එසේ නම්, තම කාර්යභාරය ඉටු කරන අධිකරණ නිලධාරීන්ට ‘අභිප්‍රාප්තිය’ එල්ල කිරීමට අවශ්‍ය වන්නේ කුමන මූලාශ්‍රවලින්ද? 105 වැනි වගන්තිය යටතේ අපහාස කිරීමේ බලතල හැරුණු විට, අධිකරණයට ඇඟිලි ගැසීම් වරදක් කිරීම සඳහා 111 සී වගන්තිය ඓතිහාසික ආනයනය කිරීම, හරියටම එවැනි තත්වයක් ගැන කථා කරයි. එම වගන්තිය, (1) උප වගන්තියේ සඳහන් වන්නේ සෑම විනිසුරුවරයෙකුම, මුලසුන හොබවන නිලධාරියෙකු, රාජ්‍ය නිලධාරියෙකු හෝ අන්තර් සම්බන්ධකයෙකු, අධිකරණ බලතල හෝ කර්තව්‍යයන් සමඟ නීතියෙන් පවරන ලද වෙනත් පුද්ගලයින් 'වෙනත් කිසිවකුගෙන්' මඟ පෙන්වීමකට හෝ වෙනත් ඇඟිලි ගැසීම්වලට යටත් නොවිය යුතු බවයි. 'අන්තර්අලියා හැර, උසස් අධිකරණයක්.

111 සී (2) වගන්තිය පවසන්නේ ‘නීතිමය අධිකාරියක්’ නොමැතිව කලින් තහනම් කර ඇති ආකාරයට මැදිහත් වන ඕනෑම පුද්ගලයෙකුට මහාධිකරණය විසින් වසරක සිර දඬුවමක්, දඩයක් මෙන්ම සිවිල් ආබාධ පැනවීමකට දඬුවම් කළ හැකි බවයි. එබැවින් එහි ප්‍රතිවිපාක බරපතල හෝ අවමන් කිරීමේ වරදකටත් වඩා වැඩි ය. අධිකරණ නිලධාරීන්ට බලහත්කාරයෙන් බලපෑම් කිරීමට උත්සාහ කරන මෙම ‘මූලද්‍රව්‍යවලට’ එරෙහිව නීතියේ සම්පූර්ණ හා බිහිසුණු අධිකාරිය ගෙන නොගියේ මන්දැයි ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ පුරවැසියන් හරියටම දැන ගැනීමට කැමතිද? ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ නීතිපතිවරයා එම ප්‍රශ්නයට පිළිතුරු දිය යුතුය, සැක නැත.
විනිසුරුවන් මත අභිලාෂයන් හෙළාදකින ‘නම් නොකළ මූලද්‍රව්‍යවලට’ සම්බන්ධ වන ශුද්ධකම කුමක්ද?

මක්නිසාද යත්, විවිධ අවස්ථාවලදී අපහාස කිරීමේ චෝදනාවට ලක් වූ විවේචකයින් සහ වෙනත් අය සමඟ පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරයෙකු නීතියෙන් දඬුවම් කරනු ලැබුවහොත්, ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාව ඊටත් වඩා දරුණු ලෙස උල්ලංඝණය කරන ‘නම් නොකළ කොටස්වලට’ කුමන ශුද්ධකමක් ද? එවැනි ‘මූලද්‍රව්‍යවල’ ආරක්ෂකයා කවුද? සෘජුව කිවහොත්, අධිකරණ නිලධාරීන්ට ඔවුන්ගේ අධිකරණ කාර්යභාරය ඉටු කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් නිකුත් කරන ලද අනතුරු ඇඟවීම් දණ්ඩමුක්තියේ සංස්කෘතිය යටපත් කරයි, එය සිවිල් පරිපාලන වර්ණාවලියේ කාර්යාලවලට පත් කිරීමට ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ සුදුසු යැයි දුටු හමුදා නිලධාරීන් විසින් ශක්තිමත් කරනු ලැබේ.

නිපුණතාවයේ සහ විනයෙහි ආදර්ශයන් ලෙස, ඉතා ඉක්මනින් ඊට පටහැනිව කටුක ලෙස ඉගෙන ගැනීමට හැකි වන පරිදි, එම හමුදාමය ‘අල්ලා ගැනීම’ අපි සාදරයෙන් පිළිගත්තෙමු. එසේ වුව ද, ‘විරෝධතා නඩු’ හසුරුවන විනිසුරුවරුන්ට ප්‍රකාශිත තර්ජන එල්ල කිරීම, නිදහසින් පසු ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ අධිකරණ ඉතිහාසයේ තවත් අපකීර්තිමත් ප්‍රථම අවස්ථාවකි. මෙය මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ ජනාධිපති ධූරයේ දෙවන වාරයේ (2013) පොදු අපරාධකරුවෙකුට සමාන අගවිනිසුරුවරයෙකුගේ නින්දිත දෝෂාභියෝගය ආසන්නයෙන් පසුවය.
මේ ජාතියේ අධිකරණ පද්ධතිය ගැන එතරම් දැනුමක් නැති අයට, රාජපක්ෂලා මේ රාජ්‍යයට තොග වශයෙන් වගකිව යුතු නැති බවද මතක් කර දිය යුතුය.

ජාත්‍යන්තර නීතීඥ සංගමය (IBA) ඇතුළු ගෝලීය අධිකරණ නිරීක්ෂකයින් විසින් අගවිනිසුරු කාර්යාලය විසින් දැඩි ලෙස නිෂේධාත්මක පරීක්ෂාවට ලක් කිරීමත් සමඟ චන්ද්‍රිකා කුමාරතුංග ජනාධිපතිනිය යටතේ අධිකරණ ආයතනයේ පිරිහීම වේගවත් විය. වඩාත් මෑතක දී සිරිසේන-වික්‍රමසිංහ රෙජීමය 19 වැනි ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනය සමඟ දේශපාලන බල කෙළි සෙල්ලම් කිරීමට ප්‍රිය කරමින් ව්‍යවස්ථාමය රාජ්‍යය ප්‍රතිෂ්ඨාපනය කිරීමේ ස්වර්ණමය අවස්ථාවක් අත්හැර දැමීය.
එබැවින් වත්මන් අගමැති රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ මූල්‍ය අර්බුදයට වත්මන් රජය වගකිව යුතු බවට ජාත්‍යන්තර මාධ්‍ය හමුවේ ප්‍රබෝධමත්ව ප්‍රකාශ කරන විට ඔහු නිවැරදි වන්නේ අර්ධ වශයෙන් පමණි.

2015-2019 කාලය තුළ ඔහුගේ නායකත්වයෙන් සොයාගත හැකි වැරදි ක්‍රියා නාමාවලිය දිගු හා බිහිසුණු ය. ලංකාවේ සියලුම දේශපාලකයින් මෙන්ම මේ අගමැතිවරයාද හිස වැල්ලේ වළලන සැබෑ පැස්බරෙකි. එය ලබා දෙමින්, ලෙවියාතන් රාජ්‍යය එහි වඩාත්ම ප්‍රචණ්ඩකාරී සහ නිෂේධාත්මක ස්වරූපයෙන් පිළිබිඹු කරමින්, රාජපක්ෂ අතිරික්තයන් පිටුපස ඇත්තේ, ඔවුන් පිටුපස ඇති නපුරු ම්ලේච්ඡ බලයේ වාතයයි.

මෙම නඩුවේ අරාජිකත්වය, රාජ්යය විසින් මෙහෙයවනු ලැබේ. BASL මිසයිලවලට දින කිහිපයකට පෙර පොලිස්පතිවරයා විසින් මහජන ආරක්‍ෂක අමාත්‍යාංශයේ ලේකම්වරයා වෙත යැවූ ලිපියක් උපායශීලී ලෙස මාධ්‍ය වෙත කාන්දු වීම මගින් එය නැවතත් පෙන්නුම් කෙරේ.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ පොලිස් අණදෙන ව්‍යුහයේ විනාශකාරී බිඳවැටීම ගෙනහැර දක්වමින් පොලිස්පතිවරයා දන්වා සිටියේ සම්මුඛ පරීක්ෂණ සඳහා පෙනී නොසිට පොලිස් නිලධාරීන් 120 දෙනෙකු පොලිස් ස්ථාන භාර නිලධාරීන් ලෙස පත් කර ඇති බවයි. මේකේ තව තියෙනවා. සම්මුඛ පරීක්ෂණවලට පෙනී සිටි නමුත් උසස්වීම් සඳහා නිර්දේශ නොවූ නිලධාරීන් 3 දෙනෙකු පත් කර තිබුණේ නොසලකා ප්‍රධාන ස්ථාන සඳහා පත් කළ අයට (පුදුමයට කරුණක් නොව) පළපුරුද්දක් නොමැත.

මෙම හෙළිදරව් කිරීම් දෙසතියකට පෙර අමාත්‍යවරුන්ගේ නිවාස ගිනි තැබීම, දේශපාලනඥයන්ට සහ දේපළවලට එල්ල වූ ප්‍රහාර පිටුපස සැඟවුණු කඩාකප්පල්කාරී හස්තයන් කොතෙක් දුරට ක්‍රියාත්මක වී ඇත්ද යන්න සම්බන්ධයෙන් අසන ප්‍රශ්නවලට සම්බන්ධ වේ.

2019 පාස්කු ඉරිදා පල්ලි සහ හෝටල් පුපුරුවා හැරීමේ ‘මහ මෝලකරු’ (මහ මොළකරු) හඳුනා ගැනීමට අසමත් වීම තරම් මෙම ප්‍රශ්නවලට පිළිතුරු නොලැබෙනු ඇත. මෙම ප්‍රහේලිකා සියල්ලම සැබෑ දක්ෂතාවයකින් සංකීර්ණ ලෙස බැඳී ඇති අතර, කුණු වූ ළූණු ස්ථර ගණනාවක් ඉවත් කිරීම අවශ්‍ය වේ. නීතිය ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමේ ප්‍රධානීන්, රාජ්‍ය අභිචෝදක දෙපාර්තුමේන්තුව සහ සෑම තැනකම පවතින විමර්ශන කොමිෂන් සභා විසින් සිවිල් වින්දිතයන් සඳහා යුක්තිය පිළිබඳ සහතික කිරීම් නිෂ්ඵල වනු ඇතැයි අපට නිශ්චිතවම අනාවැකි කිව හැකිය. ඇමතිවරුන්ට සහ දේශපාලනඥයන්ට කෙසේ වෙතත් වන්දි ගෙවනු ඇත.

මේ වන විටත් රාජ්‍යය සිය මූලික කර්තව්‍යයන්ගෙන් පසු බසිද්දී, නීතියේ ආධිපත්‍යය පිළිබඳ මෙම පාඩම් ඉගෙන ගෙන නැත. ජාතියේ අධිකරණය සැලකිල්ලක් දක්වන ‘චීන කඩයක ගොනා’ මෙන් ඝෝෂා කිරීම, මංමුලා සහගත බංකොලොත් භාවයේ ප්‍රතිවිපාක මඟහරවා ගැනීමට ජාත්‍යන්තර ණය දෙන්නන්ගෙන් ආයාචනා කරන රජයක් ගැන හොඳින් පිළිබිඹු නොවේ.
මෙම ඉරණම පුරවැසියා මතට ​​ගෙන ආවේ තක්කඩි, කෑදර සහ වාර්ගික උපාමාරු දැමීමේ දේශපාලන අණක් සහ යක්ෂයා කුමක් දැයි දැකීමට අපගේ ගැඹුරු නොහැකියාව මගිනි.
ඔබේ සමාජ ශාලා සහ ලිපි ලියන මැරයන් පාලනය කරන්න, මෙම රජයට නිශ්චිත කොන්දේසි වලින් කිව යුතුය.

ta.title: இலங்கையில் ஆழமான அரசின் முகவர்களால் அரசியலமைப்பு அரசை பல தசாப்தங்களாக நாசகாரமாக கைப்பற்றியது
body_lines: ஆழமான அரசின் முகவர்களால் அரசியலமைப்பு அரசை பல தசாப்தங்களாக நாசகாரமாக கைப்பற்றியதை நன்கு அறிந்த இலங்கை பார்வையாளர்களுக்கு இந்த வாரம் இரண்டு அசாதாரண முன்னேற்றங்கள் ஆர்வமாக இருக்கலாம்.

முதலாவதாக, இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம், இந்த வெள்ளிக்கிழமை பிரதம நீதியரசர் மற்றும் சட்டமா அதிபரிடம், 'நீதிபதிகள் மற்றும் சட்ட அங்கத்தவர்கள் மீது பொய்யான குற்றச்சாட்டுகளை சுமத்தி நீதித்துறையையும் சட்டத்தரணிகளையும் இழிவுபடுத்தும் 'சில தரப்பினரின் ஒருங்கிணைந்த முயற்சி' தொடர்பாக கடுமையான கவலையை வெளிப்படுத்தியுள்ளது. வன்முறையை ஊக்குவிக்கும் தொழில்' என்பது குறிப்பிடத்தக்கது.

வழக்கத்திற்கு மாறாக, 'இந்த கூறுகள்,' (பெயரிடப்படாதது, வெளிப்படையாக வழக்காக இருக்க விரும்புகிறது) என்று எச்சரிக்கப்பட்டது, 'போராட்டங்கள் தொடர்பான நிலுவையில் உள்ள வழக்குகளில் நீதித்துறை அதிகாரிகளால் செய்யப்பட்ட உத்தரவுகளை அவமதிக்கும் அளவிற்கு சென்றுள்ளது.' பார் கேட்டுள்ளது. இந்த 'பெயரிடப்படாத கூறுகள்' நீதிமன்ற அவமதிப்புக்காக அரசியலமைப்பின் 105 வது பிரிவின் அடிப்படையில் கையாளப்பட வேண்டும்.

அரசியலமைப்பின் 111 சி பிரிவின் கீழ் நீதித்துறையில் சட்டவிரோதமாக தலையிடுவதற்கு பொறுப்பான எவருக்கும் வழக்குத் தொடருமாறு அட்டர்னி ஜெனரல் கேட்டுக் கொள்ளப்பட்டார். இங்கே, நிச்சயமாக, உடனடி மற்றும் காப்புரிமை முரண்பாடு உள்ளது.

இலங்கையில் உள்ள பெரும்பான்மையான நீதிபதிகள், வழக்கறிஞர்கள் ஊழல்வாதிகள் என்று தளர்வாக வகைப்படுத்தும் தற்காலிக கருத்துகளின் விளைவாக, எதிர்க்கட்சி நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் ஒருவர் அவமதிப்பு செய்ததற்காக நான்கு ஆண்டுகள் சிறைக்கு அனுப்பப்பட்டார் என்பது நாம் நினைவிருக்கலாம். அவர் இன்னும் தனது பதவிக் காலத்தை அனுபவித்து வருகிறார்.

இதற்கு நேர்மாறாக, பொது ஒழுங்கின் அளவுகோலில் எதிர்ப்பு தெரிவிக்கும் உரிமை மற்றும் குடிமக்களின் பேச்சு மற்றும் வெளிப்பாடு உரிமை ஆகியவற்றை எடைபோடும் நீதித்துறை அதிகாரிகளின் மீது 'பெயரிடப்படாத கூறுகள்' செலுத்தும் அபிலாஷைகளுக்கு எவ்வளவு அதிக ஈர்ப்பு உள்ளது? நீதிபதிகள், முக்கியமாக கீழ்நிலை நீதிமன்றங்கள், கடந்த கொந்தளிப்பான வாரங்களில் இந்த செயல்பாடுகளை தவறாமல் சரியான முறையில் செய்து வருகின்றனர். அரசியல் குண்டர்களால் அமைதியான எதிர்ப்பாளர்களைத் தூண்டிவிடாமல் தாக்கியதைத் தொடர்ந்து நாடு குழப்பத்தில் மூழ்கியுள்ளதால், ஒவ்வொரு அவுன்ஸ் நீதித்துறை விவேகமும் கட்டுப்பாடும் கோரப்பட்டுள்ளது.

நீதித்துறையின் தலையீடுகள் சில சந்தர்ப்பங்களில் வன்முறையைத் தடுத்துள்ளன, அதே நேரத்தில் இந்த நாட்டில் முழு அளவிலான உள்நாட்டு மோதல்கள் தவிர்க்கப்பட்டுள்ளன. மிக முக்கியமாக, இது நீதிமன்ற அமைப்பின் மீது பொதுமக்களின் நம்பிக்கையை வலுப்படுத்தியுள்ளது மற்றும் அரசியலமைப்பு மீறல்களுக்கு எதிராக நிற்கும் நீதிபதிகள் மற்றும் வழக்கறிஞர்களின் திறனை வலுப்படுத்தியுள்ளது. அரசியல் கட்டளை (ஜனாதிபதி அலுவலகம் மற்றும் பிரதமர் அலுவலகம்) வன்முறையைத் தடுப்பதில் அதன் பங்கை முதன்முதலில் கைவிடுவது மட்டுமல்லாமல், அதற்குத் தீவிரமாக உடந்தையாகவும் மாறியதால் அது மிகவும் முக்கியமானது.

அப்படியானால், நீதித்துறை அதிகாரிகள் தங்கள் பங்கை நிறைவேற்றுவதில் 'அபிமானம்' காட்ட வேண்டிய அவசியம் என்ன? சட்டப்பிரிவு 105இன் கீழ் அவமதிப்பு அதிகாரங்களைத் தவிர, நீதித்துறையில் தலையிடுவதில் 111C விதியின் வரலாற்று இறக்குமதி, ஒரு குற்றம், துல்லியமாக அத்தகைய சூழ்நிலையைப் பற்றி பேசுகிறது. அந்த கட்டுரை, துணைக் கட்டுரை (1) இல் உள்ள ஒவ்வொரு நீதிபதி, தலைமை அதிகாரி, பொது அதிகாரி அல்லது இடைநிலை, நீதித்துறை அதிகாரங்கள் அல்லது செயல்பாடுகளுடன் சட்டத்தால் ஒப்படைக்கப்பட்ட பிற நபர்கள் 'மற்ற எந்த நபரிடமிருந்தும் வழிநடத்துதல் அல்லது பிற குறுக்கீடுகளுக்கு உட்படுத்தப்பட மாட்டார்கள். 'இன்டெராலியாவைத் தவிர, உயர் நீதிமன்றம்.

சட்டப்பிரிவு 111 சி (2) கூறுகிறது, முன்பு தடைசெய்யப்பட்ட முறையில் ‘சட்ட அதிகாரம்’ இல்லாமல் தலையிடும் எந்தவொரு நபரும், உயர் நீதிமன்றத்தால் ஒரு வருட சிறைத்தண்டனை, அபராதம் மற்றும் குடிமை குறைபாடுகள் விதிக்கப்படலாம் என்று கூறுகிறது. எனவே அதன் விளைவுகள் கடுமையானதாகவோ அல்லது அவமதிப்பு குற்றத்தை விட அதிகமாகவோ இருக்கும். நீதித்துறை அதிகாரிகளை வற்புறுத்த முயற்சிக்கும் இந்த ‘கூறுகளுக்கு’ எதிராக சட்டத்தின் முழுமையான மற்றும் பயங்கரமான அதிகாரம் ஏன் கொண்டு வரப்படவில்லை என்பதை இலங்கையின் குடிமக்கள் துல்லியமாக அறிய விரும்புகிறார்கள்? என்ற கேள்விக்கு இலங்கையின் சட்டமா அதிபர் பதிலளிக்க வேண்டும் என்பதில் சந்தேகமில்லை.
'பெயரிடப்படாத கூறுகள்' நீதிபதிகள் மீது அபிப்பிராயங்களை வெளிப்படுத்துவதில் என்ன புனிதத்தன்மை இணைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது?

ஒரு நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் சட்டத்தால் தண்டிக்கப்படுகிறார் என்றால், பல்வேறு சமயங்களில் அவமதிப்புக் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்ட விமர்சகர்கள் மற்றும் பிறருடன் சேர்ந்து, அரசியலமைப்பை மிக மோசமாக மீறும் 'பெயரிடப்படாத கூறுகளுக்கு' என்ன புனிதம் இருக்கிறது? இப்படிப்பட்ட ‘உறுப்பு’களின் பாதுகாவலர் யார்? வெளிப்படையாகச் சொல்வதானால், நீதித்துறை அதிகாரிகளுக்கு அவர்களின் நீதித்துறைப் பாத்திரத்தை நிறைவேற்றுவது தொடர்பாக விடுக்கப்பட்ட எச்சரிக்கைகள் தண்டனையிலிருந்து விடுபடும் கலாச்சாரத்தை அடிக்கோடிட்டுக் காட்டுகின்றன, இது ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ சிவில் நிர்வாக ஸ்பெக்ட்ரம் முழுவதிலும் உள்ள அலுவலகங்களுக்கு நியமனம் செய்யத் தகுந்ததாகக் கருதிய இராணுவ அதிகாரிகளால் வலுப்படுத்தப்பட்டது.

மாநிலத்தின் இராணுவப் ‘பிடிப்பு’ என்பதை நாங்கள் வரவேற்றோம், கண்களை மூடிக்கொண்டு, திறமை மற்றும் ஒழுக்கத்தின் முன்மாதிரியாக, விரைவில் மாறாக கசப்பாக கற்றுக்கொள்ளலாம். அப்படியிருந்தும், 'எதிர்ப்பு வழக்குகளை' கையாளும் நீதிபதிகளுக்கு வெளிப்படையான அச்சுறுத்தல்கள் இலங்கையின் சுதந்திரத்திற்குப் பிந்தைய நீதித்துறை வரலாற்றில் மற்றொரு பெருமைக்குரிய முதல் நிகழ்வாகும். மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச ஜனாதிபதியின் இரண்டாவது தவணையின் போது (2013) ஒரு சாதாரண குற்றவாளிக்கு நிகரான பதவியில் இருக்கும் பிரதம நீதியரசர் ஒருவரின் அவமானகரமான பதவி நீக்கம் செய்யப்பட்டதைத் தொடர்ந்து இது நடந்துள்ளது.
இந்த தேசத்தின் நீதித்துறையின் அவலங்களைப் பற்றி அதிகம் அறியாதவர்களுக்கு, இந்த அரசுக்கு ராஜபக்ஷக்கள் மொத்தப் பொறுப்பு அல்ல என்பதையும் நினைவுபடுத்த வேண்டும்.

சர்வதேச சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் (IBA) உட்பட உலகளாவிய நீதித்துறை கண்காணிப்பாளர்களால் ஆழமான எதிர்மறையான ஆய்வுக்கு பிரதம நீதியரசர் அலுவலகம் மூலம் சந்திரிகா குமாரதுங்க ஜனாதிபதியின் கீழ் நீதித்துறை நிறுவனத்தின் சீரழிவு வேகம் அதிகரித்தது. மிக சமீபத்தில், சிறிசேன-விக்கிரமசிங்க ஆட்சியானது அரசியலமைப்பு அரசை மீட்டெடுப்பதற்கான பொன்னான வாய்ப்பை கைவிட்டது, அரசியலமைப்பின் 19 வது திருத்தத்துடன் அரசியல் அதிகார விளையாட்டுகளை விளையாட விரும்புகிறது.
எனவே இலங்கையின் நிதி நெருக்கடிக்கு தற்போதைய அரசாங்கமே பொறுப்பு என்று தற்போதைய பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க சர்வதேச ஊடகங்களுக்கு மலர்ச்சியுடன் கூறும்போது, ​​அவர் ஓரளவுக்கு மட்டுமே சரியானவர்.

2015-2019 காலப்பகுதியில் அவரது தலைமையின் தவறான செயல்களின் பட்டியல் நீண்ட மற்றும் பயங்கரமானது. அனைத்து இலங்கை அரசியல்வாதிகளைப் போலவே, இந்த பிரதமரும் மணலில் தலையை புதைத்துக்கொண்ட ஒரு உண்மையான தீக்கோழி. இருப்பினும், ராஜபக்சவின் அத்துமீறல்கள், லெவியதன் அரசை அதன் மிக வன்முறையான மற்றும் எதிர்மறையான வடிவில் பிரதிபலிக்கும் மோசமான மிருகத்தனமான சக்தியின் காற்று அவர்களுக்குப் பின்னால் உள்ளது.

இந்த வழக்கில் அராஜகம், அரசால் உந்தப்படுகிறது. BASL தாக்குதலுக்கு சில நாட்களுக்கு முன்னர், பொலிஸ் மா அதிபர் (IGP) பொது பாதுகாப்பு அமைச்சின் செயலாளருக்கு எழுதிய கடிதம் மூலோபாய ரீதியாக ஊடகங்களுக்கு கசிந்தது என்பதன் மூலம் அது மீண்டும் எடுத்துக்காட்டுகிறது.

இலங்கையின் பொலிஸ் கட்டளைக் கட்டமைப்புகளின் அழிவுகரமான சிதைவைக் கோடிட்டுக் காட்டிய பொலிஸ் மா அதிபர், நேர்முகத் தேர்வுக்கு ஆஜராகாமல் 120 பொலிஸ் அதிகாரிகள் பொலிஸ் நிலையப் பொறுப்பதிகாரிகளாக நியமிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளதாகத் தெரிவித்தார். இதற்கு மேலும் உள்ளது. நேர்முகத் தேர்வில் கலந்துகொண்ட 3 அதிகாரிகள், பதவி உயர்வுக்கு பரிந்துரைக்கப்படாத நிலையில், முக்கிய நிலையங்களுக்கு நியமிக்கப்பட்டவர்கள் (ஆச்சரியப்படத்தக்க வகையில்) அனுபவம் இல்லாதவர்கள்.

இரண்டு வாரங்களுக்கு முன்னர் அமைச்சர்களின் வீடுகள் எரிப்பு, அரசியல்வாதிகள் மற்றும் சொத்துக்கள் மீதான தாக்குதல்களின் பின்னணியில் மறைந்திருந்த நாசகார கரங்கள் எந்த அளவிற்கு இருந்தன என்பது தொடர்பான கேள்விகளுடன் இந்த வெளிப்பாடுகள் இணைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன.

2019 ஈஸ்டர் ஞாயிறு அன்று தேவாலயங்கள் மற்றும் ஹோட்டல்களை 'உள்நாட்டு' ஜிஹாதிகளால் தகர்க்கப்பட்ட 'மஹா மொளகரு' (தலைமை மூளை) அடையாளம் காணத் தவறியதால் இந்தக் கேள்விகளுக்கு விடை கிடைக்காமல் போகும். இந்த புதிர்கள் அனைத்தும் உண்மையான திறமையுடன் சிக்கலான முறையில் பிணைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன, அழுகிய வெங்காயத்தின் பல அடுக்குகளை அப்படியே உரிக்க வேண்டும். சட்ட அமலாக்கத் தலைவர்கள், அரசு வழக்குரைஞர் துறை மற்றும் எங்கும் நிறைந்த விசாரணைக் கமிஷன்கள் ஆகியவற்றின் தலைவர்களால் பாதிக்கப்பட்ட பொதுமக்களுக்கு நீதிக்கான உத்தரவாதங்கள் வீணாகிவிடும் என்பதை நாம் உறுதியாகக் கணிக்க முடியும். எனினும் அமைச்சர்கள் மற்றும் அரசியல்வாதிகளுக்கு இழப்பீடு வழங்கப்படும்.

இப்போதும் அரசு தனது அடிப்படை செயல்பாடுகளில் இருந்து பின்வாங்கினாலும், சட்டத்தின் இந்த பாடங்கள் கற்றுக்கொள்ளப்படவில்லை. நாட்டின் நீதித்துறை சம்பந்தப்பட்ட 'சீனாக் கடையில் காளைகள்' போன்ற கொச்சைப்படுத்துதல், அவநம்பிக்கையான திவால்தன்மையின் விளைவுகளைத் தவிர்க்க சர்வதேச கடன் வழங்குபவர்களிடம் அரசாங்கம் கெஞ்சுவதைப் பற்றி நன்றாகப் பிரதிபலிக்கவில்லை.
இந்த விதி குடிமக்கள் மீது ஒரு வளைந்த, பேராசை மற்றும் வகுப்புவாத கையாளுதல் அரசியல் கட்டளையால் கொண்டு வரப்பட்டது, அதனுடன் பிசாசை என்னவென்று பார்க்க நமது ஆழ்ந்த இயலாமை.
உங்களின் கிளப் மற்றும் கடிதம் எழுதும் குண்டர்களை கட்டுப்படுத்துங்கள்.

en.title: The decades-long subversive capture of the Constitutional State by agents of the Deep State in Sri Lanka
body_lines: Two extraordinary developments this week may be of interest to Sri Lanka watchers familiar with the decades-long subversive capture of the Constitutional State by agents of the Deep State.

First, the Bar Association of Sri Lanka’s expression of strongly worded concern to the Chief Justice and the Attorney General this Friday, regarding ‘a concerted effort by certain parties’ to discredit the judiciary and the Bar by ‘falsely accusing judges and members of the legal profession of promoting violence,’ is significant.

Extraordinarily it was warned that ‘these elements,’ (unnamed as the Bar preferred to be the case apparently), had gone so far as to cast ‘aspersions on orders made by judicial officers in pending cases relating to protests.’The Bar has asked for these ‘unnamed elements’ to be dealt with for contempt of court in terms of Article 105 of the Constitution.

The Attorney General was also asked to prosecute any responsible for unlawful interference with the judiciary under Article 111C of the Constitution. There is, of course, an immediate and patent irony here.

As we may recall, an Opposition Member of Parliament was sent to jail for four years for committing contempt as a result of ad hoc comments loosely categorising ‘a majority’ of judges, lawyers in Sri Lanka as corrupt. He is still serving his term.

In contrast, how much more gravity attaches to aspersions cast by ‘unnamed elements’ on judicial officers weighing the right to protest and the right to speech and expression of citizens in the scales of public order? Judges, mainly of the subordinate courts, have been performing these functions in an impeccably proper manner during the past tumultuous weeks. Every ounce of judicial discretion and restraint has been called for, as the nation tipped over into chaos following unprovoked assaults of peaceful protestors by political goons.

Judicial interventions have prevented violence in some instances while full scale civil conflict in this country has been avoided in others. Most importantly, this has strengthened public confidence in the court system and the ability of both judges and lawyers to stand up to violations of the Constitution. That has been crucial as the political command (the Office of the President and the Office of the Prime Minister) not only abdicated its role in preventing the violence in the first instance but also became acutely complicit in the same.

So from what springs this need to ‘cast aspersions’ on judicial officers performing their role? Quite apart from contempt powers under Article 105, the historic import of Article 111C in making interference with the judiciary, an offence, speaks to precisely such a situation. That Article, in sub article (1) is to the effect that every judge, presiding officer, public officer or interalia, other persons entrusted by law with judicial powers or functions shall not be subjected to direction or other interference proceeding from ‘any other person’ other than interalia, a superior court.

Article 111C (2) says that any person who, without ‘legal authority’ interferes in the manner prohibited previously, may be punishable by the High Court on a jail term of one year, a fine as well as the imposition of civic disabilities. Its consequences are therefore as severe or even more so than an offence of contempt. Citizens of Sri Lanka would like to know exactly why the full and awful authority of the law has not been brought against these ‘elements’ attempting to coerce judicial officers? The Attorney General of Sri Lanka must answer to that question, no doubt.
What sanctity attaches to ‘unnamed elements’ casting aspersions on judges?

For if a Member of Parliament is punished by the law, along with critics and others accused of contempt at various times, what sanctity attaches to ‘unnamed elements’ violating the Constitution far more egregiously? Who is the protector of such ‘elements’? Put bluntly,  warnings issued to judicial officers in regard to performing their judicial role underpins the culture of impunity, bolstered by military officers who President Gotabaya Rajapaksa has seen fit to appoint to offices across the civil administration spectrum.

We welcomed that military ‘capture’ of the State, eyes wide shut, as models of competency and discipline, soon to learn bitterly to the contrary. Even so, overt threats to judges handling ‘protest cases’ is another inglorious first in Sri Lanka’s post-independence judicial history. This follows close upon the disgraceful impeachment of a sitting Chief Justice akin to a common criminal during the second term of the Mahinda Rajapaksa Presidency (2013).
For those less acquainted with the travails of this nation’s judicial system, it must also be reminded that the Rajapaksas are not wholesale responsible for this state.

The deterioration of the judicial institution increased pace under the Chandrika Kumaratunga Presidency with the Office of the Chief Justice attracting deeply negative scrutiny by global judicial monitors including the International Bar Association (IBA). More recently, the Sirisena-Wickremesinghe regime abandoned a golden opportunity to restore the Constitutional State, preferring to play political power games with the 19th Amendment to the Constitution.

So when the current Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe pronounces with a flourish to the international media that the present Government is responsible for Sri Lanka’s financial crisis, he is only partly right.

The catalogue of misdeeds traceable to his leadership during 2015-2019 are long and horrific. Like all Sri Lankan politicians, this Prime Minister too is a veritable ostrich, burying his head in the sand. Granting that, Rajapaksa excesses have nevertheless, the air of nasty brutish power behind them, reflecting the Leviathan State in its most violent and negative form.

Anarchy in this case, is propelled by the State. That is again exemplified by the fact that days before the BASL missives, a letter by the Inspector General of Police (IGP) to the Secretary of the Public Security Ministry was strategically leaked to the media.

Outlining the disastrous breakdown of Sri Lanka’s policing command structures, the IGP informed that 120 police officers have been appointed as Officers-in-Charge of Police Stations without appearing for interviews. There is more to this. 3 officers who did appear at the interviews but were not recommended for promotion, had been appointed regardless and those who had been appointed to key stations (unsurprisingly) lacked experience.

These disclosures link up to questions being asked regarding the extent to which hidden subversive hands were behind the burning of Minsters’ houses, the attacks on politicians and property two weeks ago.

These questions will go unanswered as much as the failure to identify the ‘maha molakaru’ (mastermind) of the 2019 Easter Sunday blowing up of churches and hotels, carried out by ‘homegrown’ jihadists. All of these puzzles are tied up intricately with true skill, requiring the peeling away of many layers of a rotten onion as it were. We can only predict with certainty that assurances of justice for civilian victims by the chiefs of law enforcement, the state prosecutor’s department and ubiquitous Commissions of Inquiry will be in vain. Ministers and politicians will however be compensated.

Even now as the State retreats from its basic functions, these lessons of the Rule of Law have not been learnt. Blustering about, like ‘bulls in a china shop’ where the nation’s judiciary is concerned does not reflect well on a Government pleading with international lenders to obviate the consequences of a desperate bankruptcy.
This fate was brought upon the citizenry by a crooked, greedy and communally manipulative political command coupled with our profound inability to see the devil for what it is.
Rein in your club-wielding and letter-writing goons, this Government must be told in no uncertain terms.
